meta.title: Use advanced techniques to develop an electronics outcome
meta.purpose: This achievement standard involves using advanced techniques to develop an electronics outcome.

grade_descriptors.N1: Evidence does not reach the minimum threshold for Achieved-level indicators.
grade_descriptors.N2: Some Achieved-level indicators are met, but not enough to satisfy all required Achieved criteria.
grade_descriptors.A3: All Achieved-level indicators are met; no Merit-level indicator meets the Merit threshold.
grade_descriptors.A4: All Achieved indicators are met and at least one Merit indicator is met, but overall performance is below the Merit threshold.
grade_descriptors.M5: All Achieved indicators are met and the Merit threshold is reached; no Excellence-level indicator meets the Excellence threshold.
grade_descriptors.M6: All Achieved indicators are met, all Merit indicators are met, and at least one Excellence indicator is met, but overall performance is below the Excellence threshold.
grade_descriptors.E7: All Achieved and Merit indicators are met and the Excellence threshold is reached, but not all Excellence indicators are met.
grade_descriptors.E8: All Achieved, Merit, and Excellence indicators are met.

omis.description: Shows a working electronics outcome developed using appropriate resources and techniques.

positive_signals: Demonstration logs showing the outcome functioning as intended
Written descriptions of the outcome operating correctly
Evidence that the outcome meets specified requirements
negative_signals: Stating intentions without providing demonstration evidence
Plans described without showing actual operation
Generic claims of functionality without specifics
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Evidence must be explicit and show operation; vague statements or plans alone are insufficient.

success_examples: develop a functional electronics outcome
The electronics outcome meets specifications
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Achieved bullets 1–2); Achieved commentary: first two paragraphs

omis.description: Provides evidence of testing and debugging using devices or strategies to ensure performance to specifications.

positive_signals: Testing expected inputs and verifying outputs
Testing boundary conditions for inputs
Testing invalid inputs and handling errors
Debugging actions that address specification failures
negative_signals: Generic assertions that 'it works' without details
Testing not linked to outcome specifications
Lack of evidence for debugging activities
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Testing must include explicit activities like expected, boundary, and invalid inputs, with debugging tied to specifications; vague claims are insufficient.

success_examples: testing and debugging to ensure that the electronics outcome performs to specifications
tested the input interface on expected analogue/digital inputs
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Achieved bullet 2); EN4 examples list

omis.description: Explains the interfaces and functions of components and systems used in the outcome.

positive_signals: Description of what each interface does in the system
Explanation of how components function together
Functional roles of systems clearly stated
Detailed descriptions beyond just naming parts
negative_signals: Listing component names without functional descriptions
Parts mentioned without explaining their roles
Vague or incomplete explanations of interfaces
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Explanation must describe what each interface/component does and how it functions within the system; mere listing of names or parts is insufficient.

success_examples: explaining the interfaces and functions of components and systems
functions of at least three interfaces/components
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Achieved bullet 3); Achieved commentary: explanation guidance

omis.description: Explains relevant implications for the electronics outcome (e.g., end-user, functionality, usability, safety).

positive_signals: Naming specific implications like end-user or safety
Explaining why each implication is relevant
Describing how implications are or could be addressed
Detailed connections between implications and outcome
negative_signals: Vague or generic mentions of implications
Listing implications without explanations
Not describing how they are addressed
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Each implication must be named, its relevance explained, and how it is addressed described; vague or generic mentions are insufficient.

success_examples: explaining relevant implications
explained at least two identified relevant implications
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Achieved bullet 4) and EN5; Achieved commentary: implications paragraph

omis.description: Uses at least two advanced techniques appropriate to the outcome such as embedded software, subsystem design, or PCB development.

positive_signals: Evidence of embedded software implementation
Subsystem design details shown
Advanced PCB development described
EEPROM storage used in the outcome
ADC functionality implemented
negative_signals: Listing possible techniques without implementation evidence
Mentioning techniques without showing how they were used
Evidence of fewer than two techniques
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Must provide explicit evidence of at least two advanced techniques being implemented; listing possibilities or names alone is insufficient.

success_examples: They have used at least two advanced techniques
Examples of advanced techniques may include: using embedded software, subsystem design, or ADC
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN3 examples; Achieved commentary: advanced techniques evidence

omis.description: Identifies what the electronics outcome does and how components work together to achieve this behaviour.

positive_signals: Clear identification of the system's overall behaviour
Explanation of how components interact to achieve behaviour
Description of component functions in context
Integration of behaviour and function explanations
negative_signals: Describing components in isolation without interaction
Only stating what the system does without how
Only explaining functions without linking to behaviour
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Must include both what the system does (behaviour) and how components interact (function); isolated descriptions are insufficient.

success_examples: identifying the behaviour and function of the electronics outcome
explained what the system will do and how the components work together
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Merit bullet 1); Merit commentary: first paragraph

omis.description: Shows testing and modifying actions that enhance the reliability of the electronics outcome.

positive_signals: Testing linked to specific modifications for reliability
Changing board layout to improve reliability
Improving component selection based on testing
Using testing devices or strategies to guide changes
negative_signals: Correcting only small code errors without broader impact
Testing performed without resulting modifications
Modifications not connected to enhancing reliability
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Testing and modifying must be linked and aimed at enhancing reliability; minor code fixes alone are insufficient.

success_examples: testing and modifying to ensure reliability
made significant improvements by changing components and reorganising the circuit layout
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Merit bullet 2); EN4 examples; Merit commentary: testing/modifying reliability

omis.description: Evaluates the choice of components and systems used by comparing alternatives and providing reasons for choices.

positive_signals: Comparison of alternative components or systems
Reasoning provided for why choices were made
Evaluative statements on advantages and disadvantages
Links between evaluation and selected options
negative_signals: Simple description of components without comparison
No reasoning given for choices
Listing options without evaluative analysis
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Must include comparison of alternatives and reasoning for choices; mere description is insufficient.

success_examples: evaluating the choice of components and systems used
comparing these with other options and providing reasons
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Merit bullet 3); Merit commentary: evaluation guidance

omis.description: Shows how at least two relevant implications have been addressed in the outcome through design or development decisions.

positive_signals: Connecting specific implications to design changes
Evidence of addressing implications through decisions
At least two implications shown with connections
Links between implications and outcome modifications
negative_signals: Listing implications without showing how addressed
No evidence of design or development decisions
Fewer than two implications connected
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Must connect implications to implemented changes or decisions; mere listing is insufficient.

success_examples: addressing relevant implications
shown how their electronics outcome addresses at least two relevant implications
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Merit bullet 4); Merit commentary: implications addressed

omis.description: Undertakes iterative improvement throughout design, development, and testing to produce a high-quality outcome.

positive_signals: Multiple cycles of testing and refinement shown
Improvements throughout design and development stages
Deliberate iterations with evidence of changes
Ongoing cycles rather than single fixes
negative_signals: Evidence of only a single improvement
Linear process without repeated cycles
No demonstration of iterative refinement
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Must show multiple deliberate cycles of testing and refinement; a single improvement or linear process is insufficient.

success_examples: undertaking iterative improvement throughout the design, development and testing process
multiple instances of ongoing design, development and testing
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Excellence bullet 1); Excellence commentary: iterative improvement

omis.description: Justifies the choice of components and systems used with reference to investigations, comparisons, or outcomes achieved.

positive_signals: Reasoned justification for component choices
Reference to investigations or comparisons
Outcomes achieved supporting the choices
Evidence-based reasoning for selections
negative_signals: Listing advantages without linking to decisions
No reference to investigations or comparisons
Vague justifications without support
evidence_guide.common_confusion: Justification must be reasoned and supported by evidence, investigations, or comparisons; mere listing of advantages is insufficient.

success_examples: justifying the choice of components and systems used
investigating and comparing alternative components and interfaces
omis.source_ref: AS PDF p2 EN2 (Excellence bullet 2); Excellence commentary: justification example

change_log: Integrated EN3 and EN4 examples from AS91894 to refine detection hints for advanced techniques and testing/modifying/debugging.
Updated weightings for reliability and advanced technique OMIs to 2 due to critical presence across Merit and Achieved commentary.
Clarified scope of 'relevant implications' and refined iterative improvement evidence for alignment with Excellence commentary.
